item.title: Has Trump Gone Full ‘Mob Boss’?
item.description: The president’s $1.8 billion slush fund is causing further cracks in the Republican Party.

item.title: The Moms Delivered for Trump. Now He’s Scamming Them.
item.description: The moms are not happy.

item.title: Americans Don’t Talk to Their Neighbors. That’s a Problem for Kids.
item.description: ‘Stranger danger’ was always a misleading idea, and it’s bad for our children.

item.title: What A.I. Philanthropists Can Learn From the Gilded Age
item.description: Leave a physical legacy to future generations, not just a record of programs and disbursements.

item.title: Now Is Not the Time to Play Political Make-Believe
item.description: Voters want to dictate which offices their preferred politicians hold, what positions they take, and even where and how they live. It is fantasy football: political edition.

item.title: ‘We Should All Apply’ to Trump’s Slush Fund
item.description: Trump’s proposed $1.8 billion compensation fund tied to Jan. 6 is drawing scrutiny. On “The Opinions,” David French explains why he sees a double standard between how rioters and ordinary Americans would be treated under the system.

item.title: Firing Prevention Experts Will Not Make Us Healthy Again
item.description: The future of health advice is uncertain.

item.title: Has Trump Gone Full ‘Mob Boss’?
item.description: The president’s $1.8 billion slush fund is causing further cracks in the Republican Party.

item.title: Dan Pfeiffer Thinks Two Democrats Could Upend American Politics
item.description: Why 2026 might tell us a lot about 2028.

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Finale Didn’t Mean What It Seemed To
item.description: You’re going to miss corporate media.

item.title: There’s a Way to Stop Trump’s I.R.S. Slush Fund
item.description: There should be no payouts to Jan. 6 rioters.

item.title: Trump Is Giving ‘The Fix Was In’ New Meaning
item.description: Trump is giving “the fix was in” new meaning.

item.title: Trump Just Took Us Somewhere the Country Had Never Been Before
item.description: There’s no telling where this leads.

item.title: The Simple Answer to Taxing the Rich Is the Best Answer
item.description: Forget complex schemes. There is an easier way to get revenue from the wealthy.

item.title: Washington Is Still Trying to Rewrite Cuban History
item.description: How can Cubans move forward if they aren’t ready to reckon with their past?

item.title: The Agony Around the Democrats’ Mysterious, Ridiculous Autopsy
item.description: After this fiasco, Ken Martin, the chair of the D.N.C., should be replaced.

item.title: Court Cases About Money and Speech in Politics
item.description: Readers discuss court cases about campaign finance. Also: Computer science and the humanities.

item.title: What Silicon Valley Is Coming for Next
item.description: Hint: It comes from inside of you.

item.title: Now Give Me Money (That’s What I Want)
item.description: Trump has molded the presidency in his image. It isn’t pretty.

item.title: Pope Leo Chooses Social Justice Over Pelvic Theology
item.description: The document will address A.I. and its impact on humanity. He’s also staying the course on Catholic social teaching.

item.title: A.I. Is a Job Creator
item.description: A.I. will create more jobs than it will kill.

item.title: Your Questions About Nicholas Kristof’s Column on Palestinians and Sexual Assault
item.description: The Times offers answers to some of the pressing questions raised.

item.title: Ebola Is a Terrible Disease. I Saw It Firsthand.
item.description: The Trump administration cut the programs meant to coordinate an Ebola response.

item.title: A Black Helen of Troy? Fine. A White Obama? Not Yet.
item.description: There are good reasons white actors should not be playing Black characters in our moment

item.title: A Cattle Ranch Is Doing What Ivy League Colleges Can’t
item.description: College education does almost nothing to develop the sense that what we do in our day-to-day lives resonates with people beyond ourselves.

item.title: Kennedy’s Move to Rein In Antidepressants
item.description: Readers respond to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s effort to help Americans quit antidepressants. Also: Agonizing decisions; strangers no more.

item.title: 60 Percent of Grades at Harvard Were A’s. Enough Is Enough.
item.description: We deserve an A-minus for effort.

item.title: A Defense of a Liberal Arts Education in the Age of A.I.
item.description: Making the case for a “useless” education.

item.title: Trump Just Pardoned Himself and His Family Forever
item.description: By issuing a prohibition on federal investigations that might threaten Trump’s finances, the Justice Department has placed the president and his family in a new category.

item.title: Hollywood Ghosted the Cannes Film Festival This Year
item.description: Fewer movie stars, fewer oligarch megayachts in the bay, fewer parties and ever more desperate paparazzi.

item.title: MAHA Is No Longer Useful to Trump
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s MAHA movement has reshaped the public health debate. But if the coalition behind it begins to fracture, what comes next?

item.title: A Defense of a Liberal Arts Education in the Age of A.I.
item.description: Making the case for a ‘useless’ education.

item.title: This Is How a Party Ends Up Looking Like a Clown Car
item.description: We need leaders who tell us hard truths while insisting that the present state of our politics is not permanent.

item.title: It’s Not a Mystery Why Fertility Rates Are Declining
item.description: More and more, smaller families and a more manageable work-life balance are the goal.

item.title: Breaking the Two-Party System
item.description: America’s two-party system isn’t an accident — it is a direct result of our electoral structure. The political scientist Lee Drutman tells the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein how shifting to proportional representation could pave the way for viable third parties and give voters more choices.

item.title: Why Are Kids Using A.I. at School?
item.description: A.I. is replicating the pattern followed by every ed-tech quick fix.

item.title: Trump, the Audits and the Payout Fund
item.description: Readers respond to news that the I.R.S. would be barred from pursuing tax claims against President Trump, his family or his businesses. Also: Protecting precious places; growing native plants.

item.title: E. Jean Carroll Taught Me to Not Be Afraid
item.description: It’s not easy to make an anti-Trump documentary right now.

item.title: Where Are the Republicans Who Put America First?
item.description: The “Trump First” wing of the G.O.P. is in ascendance. The rest of the party needs to fight back.

item.title: Trump’s Slush Fund Will Reward Criminals. Americans Will Pay for It.
item.description: The president’s corruption and subversion of democratic tradition risk becoming the norm.

item.title: One Chatbot to Rule Everything
item.description: A.I. is influencing everything, from what we wear to what we read. Now it’s coming for something more intimate: human taste. On “The Opinions,” the question is what happens when A.I. becomes the filter for how we experience culture.

item.title: How I Lost a Religion
item.description: Lessons from sports fandom on why traditions fade.

item.title: Thank You, President Trump
item.description: The president’s trolling of Canada, Greenland and Venezuela over becoming America’s next state has had one positive side effect, the Opinion columnist Jamelle Bouie argues.

item.title: Hatred of Israel and the Degradation of the West
item.description: Feeding narratives to the credulous isn’t speaking truth to power.

item.title: Which Has Better Taste: A.I. or Humans?
item.description: Why Silicon Valley is obsessed with taste.

item.title: I’m the Foreign Minister of Sweden. Don’t Overestimate Russia.
item.description: We can’t afford to underestimate Russia’s strength and its ability to harm Ukraine and NATO allies, but we must not overestimate it either.

item.title: Is This the End of Close Elections?
item.description: Rampant gerrymandering and intense partisan sorting have shrunk the number of competitive House seats to a record-low. The political scientist Lee Drutman explains how on “The Ezra Klein Show.”

item.title: Kentucky Had an Easy Choice Between Massie and Trump
item.description: The president wants a loyal Republican Party — even if the country might not.

item.title: Lamenting the End of Colbert’s ‘Late Show’
item.description: Readers will miss Stephen Colbert’s “joy, wit, nonsense and sanity,” in the words of one writer. Also: President Trump’s fund; Kash Patel’s snorkeling trip; Meta’s retort.

item.title: How to End the Gerrymandering Doom Loop Forever
item.description: The political scientist Lee Drutman argues that we should switch to a system of proportional representation and put an end to our “trench warfare politics.”
